reasoning: Five years I waited for the paper that Shantanu Thakur promised when I pressed the lotus button in 2019. My roof still leaks. My grandson still goes hungry. Now they give fourteen certificates—fourteen!—just five days before voting, like a show for the booth, not a promise kept. Both the lotus people and the green flag people have forgotten that Sarojini Haldar exists. I know Shantanu is Matua like me, and once I believed in him. But I have learned not to celebrate until the paper is in my hands, and I do not have it. Biswajit Das is not even Matua, and he changes his shirt like the seasons. I am too old and too tired to vote for the same broken promise again, even if it wears a familiar face.
primary_drivers: 14 symbolic certificates delivered 5 days before election—a show, not a kept promise
Five years without the citizenship documentation that was promised in 2019
Unmet material needs: leaking roof, hungry grandchildren, while politicians fight over temples and power
